text.title: azure-compute Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for azure-compute as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: VMSS Uniform mode in old Terraform that still defaults to Uniform.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force Flexible orchestration on every new VMSS.

failure-mode.description: AKS Pod Identity is deprecated in favor of Workload Identity (OIDC + federated credentials). Agents still scaffold Pod Identity from old blog posts.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening

failure-mode.description: KEDA add-on must be enabled AFTER Workload Identity on AKS — wrong order leaves KEDA without an identity, scalers fail silently.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Enable OIDC issuer and Workload Identity before KEDA.

failure-mode.description: Container Apps: no Windows containers, no ARM64, no privileged containers. Agents reach for --privileged and the deploy fails late.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening

failure-mode.description: Container Apps revisions: setting min-replicas: 0 triggers cold starts (5-30s on first request).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: For latency-sensitive APIs use min-replicas: 1 and accept the cost.

failure-mode.description: App Service slot swap WITHOUT warmup → first requests after swap hit cold workers and 5xx.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening

failure-mode.description: VM/VMSS Spot evictions: 30-second notice via Scheduled Events; agents forget to wire the metadata endpoint and lose work mid-task.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening

failure-mode.description: AKS Cluster Autoscaler vs. Karpenter (now AKS Node Auto-Provisioning preview) — agents mix recommendations, end up with neither truly enabled.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pick one and configure it properly.

failure-mode.description: Quotas (vCPU per region, public IPs, NIC count) bite at scale. New subscriptions have absurdly low defaults; agents file ARM templates that hit quota mid-deploy and leave half-created resources.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Check quotas with az vm list-usage before deploying.

failure-mode.description: Agents pick a region by name without checking SKU availability — Standard_D4ds_v5 exists in westeurope but not in swedencentral Spot.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force a az vm list-skus -l <region> check before generating IaC.

failure-mode.description: Quota limits cause partial deploys: agents apply, hit a vCPU quota, leave half-created NICs / disks.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always run az deployment group what-if AND check az vm list-usage first.

failure-mode.description: "Subscription" vs. "tenant" vs. "management group" confusion → agents create resources in the wrong subscription.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Bind subscription ID via az account set --subscription at the very top of any script and assert via az account show.

failure-mode.description: Service principal vs. managed identity: agents default to creating a SP because samples do; SP secrets expire and rotate poorly.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Prefer Managed Identity / Workload Identity; only use SP for cross-tenant edge cases.

failure-mode.description: Soft-delete on Key Vault / Storage: agents terraform destroy and the next apply fails because the name is reserved for 7-90 days.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use --purge or rename — and document the choice.

failure-mode.description: Costs creep silently: agents add a Premium SSD + GRS Storage + always-on App Service Plan; no one notices for a billing cycle.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Wire cost projection into the PR check.
